texts.value: Transcription factor that plays a key role in neuronal differentiation: acts as a pioneer transcription factor, accessing closed chromatin to allow other factors to bind and activate neural pathways. Directly binds the E box motif (5'-CANNTG-3') on promoters and promotes transcription of neuronal genes. The combination of three transcription factors, ASCL1, POU3F2/BRN2 and MYT1L, is sufficient to reprogram fibroblasts and other somatic cells into induced neuronal (iN) cells in vitro. Plays a role at early stages of development of specific neural lineages in most regions of the CNS, and of several lineages in the PNS. Essential for the generation of olfactory and autonomic neurons. Acts synergistically with FOXN4 to specify the identity of V2b neurons rather than V2a from bipotential p2 progenitors during spinal cord neurogenesis, probably through DLL4-NOTCH signaling activation. Involved in the regulation of neuroendocrine cell development in the glandular stomach (By similarity)
Efficient DNA binding requires dimerization with another bHLH protein. Forms a heterodimer with TCF3

Genetic variations in ASCL1 may play a role in disorders of the neurocristopathy spectrum manifesting with features of Hirschsprung disease, congenital central hypoventilation syndrome, dysautonomia, in addition to neurodevelopmental issues

comments.resourceName: Atlas of Genetics and Cytogenetics in Oncology and Haematology

features.description: found in two unrelated individuals with short-segment Hirschsprung disease, dysautonomia, increased lower limb reflexes and neurodevelopmental issues; likely pathogenic

citation.title: Identification of a human achaete-scute homolog highly expressed in neuroendocrine tumors.
Complete sequencing and characterization of 21,243 full-length human cDNAs.
The status, quality, and expansion of the NIH full-length cDNA project: the Mammalian Gene Collection (MGC).
HASH-1 and E2-2 are expressed in human neuroblastoma cells and form a functional complex.
Further description of two individuals with de novo p.(Glu127Lys) missense variant in the ASCL1 gene.
